name: CrewAI
description: Framework for orchestrating role-playing, autonomous AI agents in role-based Crews or event-driven Flows

description.value: Python framework for orchestrating role-playing, autonomous AI agents that collaborate in role-based Crews or event-driven Flows to tackle complex tasks

first-release.comment: Framework completed October 2023 and published as open source on GitHub in November 2023; CrewAI Inc officially launched January 2024

pricing-model.comment: Core framework is MIT-licensed free OSS; CrewAI AMP Basic is free (50 executions/mo); CrewAI AMP Cloud is paid SaaS; CrewAI AMP Factory / Enterprise is paid self-hosted

covered-phases.comment: CrewAI is a general-purpose agent orchestration framework and does not prescribe SDLC phases; users define what their Crews or Flows cover

methodology-style.comment: Multi-agent role-playing is the core abstraction; Flows provide event-driven plan-and-execute patterns; value is primarily the orchestration harness

opinionated.comment: Users define all agents, tasks, and flow control; no prescribed workflow template

ceremony-level.comment: Scaffolding produces minimal YAML config plus a small crew.py entrypoint; no mandatory review gates or artifact ceremony

mechanization-level.comment: Single `crewai run` or `kickoff()` call executes a crew or flow end-to-end

hitl-checkpoints.comment: @human_feedback decorator on Flows pauses execution for approval/rejection/revision; checkpoints are opt-in rather than prescribed

approval-gates.comment: No prescribed approval gates in the default configuration; @human_feedback gates are user-defined

autonomy-level.comment: Default Crews and Flows run fully autonomously from kickoff to completion; any human checkpoints are opt-in

rollback-support.comment: Flow @persist saves state for replay/resume but there is no built-in rollback of generated code or external side effects; the CLI `replay` resumes from a task rather than reverting

supported-agents.comment: crewAIInc/skills is a first-party Claude Code plugin/marketplace distributing CrewAI skills. CrewAI itself is the agent runtime (not driven by external coding agents), so other tags do not apply in the RESEARCH.md sense

editor-integrations.comment: Python library invoked via the `crewai` CLI; no first-party editor extension

model-agnostic.comment: Native SDK integrations for OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Azure, AWS Bedrock; 15+ additional providers via LiteLLM (Ollama, Groq, Mistral, Hugging Face, Vertex, SageMaker, etc.)

mcp-support.comment: Agents consume MCP servers as tools via the `mcps=[]` field / MCPServerAdapter; supports stdio, SSE, and Streamable HTTP transports

artifact-formats.comment: Primary non-code artifacts are YAML (agents.yaml, tasks.yaml); scaffold also produces Markdown README and knowledge/ files

artifacts-covered.comment: Does not maintain SDLC artifacts (specs, plans, ADRs, release notes); the framework's own artifacts are agent and task configuration rather than software-lifecycle documents

artifacts-in-git.comment: agents.yaml, tasks.yaml, crew.py, tools/, knowledge/ all live in the project repo by default

language-agnostic.comment: Framework itself is Python, but workflows can target any domain or target-language codebase via tools

enforcement-mechanisms.comment: Primary enforcement is instructional via agent roles/goals/backstories; lifecycle callbacks (task_callback, step_callback) and Flow decorators allow mechanical checks

strict-phase-ordering.comment: Flows enforce user-defined event ordering via @start/@listen/@router, but the framework itself does not prescribe phases

artifact-validation.comment: agents.yaml and tasks.yaml are validated against Pydantic schemas at load time

memory-implementation.comment: Unified Memory API combines vector similarity (LanceDB default) with file-based storage under `.crewai/memory/`; custom StorageBackend protocol supported

persistent-memory.comment: Memory and Flow state persist to disk across sessions; Flows use SQLiteFlowPersistence by default

project-context-files.comment: Scaffolds project-scoped config files (agents.yaml, tasks.yaml) plus a knowledge/ folder; does not use or produce CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md style general instruction files

cost-tracking.comment: Crew/Task outputs expose token_usage (prompt/completion/total); CrewAI AMP tracing aggregates per-run cost and token metrics in the cloud; no built-in budget limits

run-history.comment: CrewAI AMP tracing stores structured run traces in the cloud (requires free account); core framework also logs task outputs via `crewai log-tasks-outputs` and Flow state persistence

telemetry-opt.comment: Anonymous telemetry is documented; can be disabled via CREWAI_DISABLE_TELEMETRY=true (added in PR #2537) or OTEL_SDK_DISABLED=true

custom-templates.comment: Users freely define agent roles, goals, backstories and task descriptions in YAML; `crewai create` scaffolds an editable template

hooks.comment: Crew and Agent expose task_callback and step_callback lifecycle hooks; Flows use @start / @listen / @router / @persist / @human_feedback decorators

sub-agents.comment: Core framework primitive: user-defined role-playing Agents with optional delegation (allow_delegation=true) and custom tools

marketplace.comment: Public repositories of shared templates and skills exist (crewAI-skills Claude Code marketplace, crewAI-examples, community-curated awesome-crewai); no formal third-party marketplace

install-mechanism.comment: Recommended: `uv tool install crewai` or `uv pip install crewai`; plain `pip install crewai` also works; requires Python 3.10-3.13

setup-time.comment: uv install + `crewai create crew <name>` completes in minutes; longer if configuring LLM credentials or AMP account

self-hosted.comment: Core OSS framework runs entirely on user infrastructure; AMP cloud features are optional

requires-account.comment: Not required for the core framework or local execution; a free CrewAI account is needed for AMP tracing and `crewai deploy`

documentation-quality.comment: Extensive docs at docs.crewai.com covering concepts (Agents, Tasks, Crews, Flows, Memory, LLMs), CLI, MCP, observability, deployment, and tools; plentiful examples via crewAI-examples

active-maintenance.comment: Release 1.14.2 on 2026-04-17 (within 4 days of research on 2026-04-21); weekly release cadence

example-projects.comment: crewAI-examples contains trip planner, landing page, stock analysis, job posting, and more; crewAI-quickstarts provides feature demos

community-channel.comment: Discord (~9k members), community.crewai.com forum, and GitHub Discussions
